project-kg.description: Repo-local project knowledge graph. Directory graph boundaries define decisions, insights, patterns, rejected approaches, tasks, workspace, sources, and self-context.

codex-provider.description: Codex provider/control package for drift checks, promotion gates, hooks, phase watchdogs, and read-only MCP surfaces.

kg-hook-runtime.description: KG hook runtime for dynamic context selection, packet validation, vocabulary mapping, and Codex hook emission.

context-selector.description: CUE contracts for transient context selection, packet shape, validation gates, and Codex emission.

kg-vocab.description: Upstream quicue KG JSON-LD vocabulary context mapping CUE KG fields and entity types to linked-data semantics; .kg/vocab/context.cue is only a local hook fallback.

drift-checker.description: Executable checker owned by the Codex provider. It derives findings from repo facts, patch facts, provider policy, and project self-context.

promo-gate.description: Admission gate derived from promotion facts and Codex provider policy.

lattice-kernel.description: Provider-agnostic lattice proof and closedness kernel.

idiomatic-patterns.description: Provider-agnostic CUE idioms and contract patterns.

codex-plugins.description: Runtime host adapters. Plugins may shim into KG hooks but do not define provider authority or context selection logic.

generated-codex.description: Generated reports, facts, and projections. Never source authority.

codex-provider-not-project-kg.statement: .kg/codex is a Codex provider/control package, not the project knowledge graph.

project-kg-is-kb.statement: .kb is the repo-local project knowledge authority.

generated-is-never-authority.statement: generated/ surfaces are projections and must not be promoted to authority without review.

plugins-are-adapters.statement: Plugins are runtime adapters, not providers unless explicitly declared.

drift-checker-is-tool.statement: drift-checker is a provider tool, not the provider.

context-packets-are-transient.statement: Context packets are transient runtime projections and must not become source authority or the next run's input.

no-generated-resolver-runtime-inputs.statement: Generated resolver fragments, prompt routes, route inventories, and context indexes are forbidden as runtime context inputs.
